body: Hi Suresh, good to hear you're keen on the corporate bulk thali idea. Your 'Weekday Lunch Thali @ ₹149' is already popular, and 'weekday lunch thali' searches are up 34% YoY. How about a corporate package: 10+ thalis @ ₹135 each? Reply YES if you'd like to activate this.
rationale: The message directly responds to the merchant's intent for a corporate bulk thali package. It leverages their existing 'Weekday Lunch Thali @ ₹149' offer and validates the concept with the 'weekday lunch thali' trend (34% YoY increase). A concrete bulk pricing (10+ thalis @ ₹135) is proposed, followed by a clear binary CTA.

Hi Riya,
कल आपकी appointment है Beauty Lounge by Renu में। हम आपका इंतज़ार कर रहे हैं!
क्या आप अपनी appointment confirm करना चाहेंगी? Reply YES/NO
rationale: This message uses a warm, friendly tone suitable for a salon, reminding Riya about her upcoming appointment. It's concise and includes a clear binary CTA for confirmation, which helps the salon manage their schedule efficiently.

body:
Hi Ramesh, summer season mein ORS demand +40% aur anti-fungal demand +45% upar ja rahi hai. Apne shelf stock levels check kar lo to avoid out-of-stock situations.
Shall I send a quick checklist for summer essentials stock? YES/NO
rationale: Addressed Ramesh directly, used precise seasonal trend data (+40% ORS, +45% anti-fungal demand) from the trigger payload, and suggested a practical shelf action (stock check) relevant to pharmacies. The tone is trustworthy and uses natural Hinglish. Ends with a clear binary CTA.

body: नमस्ते Mr. Sharma, Apollo Health Plus Pharmacy से। आपकी metformin, atorvastatin और telmisartan की दवाएं 28 अप्रैल तक खत्म हो सकती हैं। पिछली बार आपने 26 मार्च को ली थीं। क्या आप सुबह की डिलीवरी शेड्यूल करना चाहेंगे?
rationale: The message uses a precise, trustworthy tone in Hindi, addressing Mr. Sharma by name. It clearly states the specific molecules, the exact stock run-out date (April 28th), and the last refill date (March 26th) as concrete facts. It leverages the customer's preference for morning delivery and ends with a low-friction binary CTA.
